edge_hypothesis: RSI Pullback tail harvester at 8:1 R:R on 4h achieved PF=1.442 (ACCEPT) but with fragile trade count (156 trades). The RSI R:R curve is barely mapped — only 7:1 (PF=1.332, 38 trades) and 8:1 data exist. Two critical unknowns: (1) WHERE does the RSI R:R curve peak? Testing 5:1 will determine if RSI pullback follows MACD's pattern (peak at mid-range R:R) or supertrend's pattern (peak at high R:R). (2) CAN RSI PULLBACK SURVIVE ON 1H? MACD proved 1h works — MACD 6:1 on 1h achieved PF=1.460 with 170 trades (more than most 4h strategies). RSI pullback's fragility problem (38-156 trades on 4h) should be solved by 1h: 4x more bars = 4x more potential pullback entries. The rsi_pullback template uses Close > EMA_200 + EMA_50 slope + RSI <= 40 for longs. On 1h, EMA_200 represents ~8 days of price action (vs ~33 days on 4h), meaning the trend filter is more responsive. This could increase signal frequency without degrading quality — RSI oversold pullbacks are a natural timeframe-fractal pattern. Testing 5:1 and 7:1 on 1h maps the R:R curve while simultaneously solving the trade count problem. A third variant tests 5:1 on 4h to fill the 4h R:R curve gap. Regime assumption: TRENDING + RANGING (RSI pullback 8:1 is profitable in both, transitional marginal at PF=0.93). Invalidation: (1) If ALL 1h variants produce PF < 1.0, the RSI pullback signal doesn't scale to lower timeframes — it's a 4h-specific pattern. (2) If 5:1 R:R significantly outperforms 8:1 (like MACD 7:1 > MACD 8:1), RSI pullback's optimal R:R is lower than previously assumed.

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_tail_harvester_5to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/1h with 5:1 R:R (1.5x ATR stop / 7.5x ATR TP). The 1h timeframe migration test at moderate R:R. On 1h, EMA_200 covers ~8 days and EMA_50 covers ~2 days, making the trend filter more responsive than 4h. RSI_14 pullbacks to <=40 occur more frequently on 1h because intra-day volatility creates more oversold readings within uptrends. The 5:1 R:R is the lower bound — MACD's viable range starts at 3.5:1 (PF=1.347), so 5:1 is conservative. Breakeven win rate: 16.7%. Expected: 300+ trades on 1h (vs 156 on 4h at 8:1), solving the fragility problem. If PF > 1.2 with 300+ trades, RSI pullback becomes a viable 1h strategy family.
entry_long: Close > EMA_200 (price above long-term trend on 1h).
entry_short: Close < EMA_200 (price below long-term trend on 1h).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Trend filter: Close > EMA_200 (long), Close < EMA_200 (short)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (7.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 5:1. Needs 16.7% win rate to break even after fees.
1h timeframe migration test: does RSI pullback scale to lower TF with more trade count?
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.5x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend filter + EMA_50 slope — responsive on 1h timeframe
RSI_14 <= 40 pullback detection — higher frequency on 1h due to intra-day volatility
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 7.5x TP — moderate 5:1 R:R baseline for 1h curve

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_tail_harvester_7to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/1h with 7:1 R:R (1.0x ATR stop / 7.0x ATR TP). Tighter stop than the 5:1 variant — if RSI pullback works on 1h, the tight stop should cut losers faster. 7:1 is the proven MACD peak R:R on 4h. Testing 7:1 on 1h RSI pullback determines whether the optimal R:R is template-specific or universal. On 4h, rsi_pullback at 7:1 achieved PF=1.332 (ACCEPT but fragile with 38 trades). On 1h, trade count should be 4x higher (~150+), making the result statistically robust. Breakeven win rate: 12.5%.
entry_long: Close > EMA_200 (price above long-term trend on 1h).
entry_short: Close < EMA_200 (price below long-term trend on 1h).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Trend filter: Close > EMA_200 (long), Close < EMA_200 (short)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (7.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
R:R = 7:1. Needs 12.5% win rate to break even after fees.
Cross-template R:R test: 7:1 is MACD's peak — is it universal?
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend + EMA_50 slope — 1h timeframe migration test
RSI_14 <= 40 pullback — testing if 4h pullback pattern scales to 1h
ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 7.0x TP — 7:1 R:R (MACD's peak) cross-template test

variants.name: rsi_pullback_4h_tail_harvester_5to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/4h with 5:1 R:R (1.5x ATR stop / 7.5x ATR TP). Fills the 4h R:R curve gap — only 7:1 and 8:1 exist. If PF at 5:1 is higher than 8:1 (PF=1.442), RSI pullback's R:R curve peaks LOW like MACD (7:1) rather than HIGH like supertrend (8:1+). The 1.5x ATR stop gives slightly more room than the tight 1.0x used at 8:1. RSI pullback entries have inherent support (RSI is oversold relative to trend), so a slightly wider stop may reduce stop-hunting while the moderate TP captures more winners. Breakeven win rate: 16.7%. Expected: ~200 trades (wider stop captures trades that 1.0x stop would have killed).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Trend filter: Close > EMA_200 (long), Close < EMA_200 (short)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (7.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 5:1. Needs 16.7% win rate to break even after fees.
4h R:R curve gap fill: does RSI pullback peak at low R:R (like MACD) or high R:R (like supertrend)?
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.5x ATR_14 from entry.
